data.title: Israel parliament gives initial approval for 2026 budget, averting snap election for now
data.description:
By Steven Scheer

Israel's parliament gave initial approval to the 2026 state budget draft on Thursday, handing Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu a temporary political reprieve by averting the immediate prospect of an early election.

Lawmakers voted 62-55 in favor of the spending plan, which totals 662 billion shekels ($214.43 billion), excluding debt servicing, and sets a budget deficit ceiling of 3.9% of gross domestic product this year.

The budget, as well as an accompanying economic plan, still faces a difficult path to final approval amid deepening polarisation within Netanyahu's governing coalition. Under Israeli law, the budget must be passed by the end of March or parliament would automatically dissolve, triggering a snap election.

Tensions inside the coalition have simmered for more than two years, fuelled by disagreements over the war in Gaza, the ceasefire reached in October that halted the fighting, and demands by ultra-Orthodox Jewish parties for legislation exempting religious seminary students from mandatory military service.

Some ultra-Orthodox lawmakers did not vote in favour of the budget since a military conscription bill has not yet been approved.

Netanyahu's other right-wing coalition partners - as well as opposition parties - argue that ultra-Orthodox men must share the burden of military service, particularly after two years of fighting in Gaza and Lebanon in which nearly 1,000 Israeli soldiers were killed.

($1 = 3.0872 shekels)

data.title: Before the bell: Earnings ramp up, SAP drag for the DAX
data.description:
BEFORE THE BELL: EARNINGS RAMP UP, SAP DRAG FOR THE DAX

European shares were set for slight gains on Thursday following a mixed reception to U.S. Big Tech earnings and modest gains on Wall Street after the Fed kept rates unchanged and gave little indication when borrowing costs might fall again.

Contracts on the EuroSTOXX 50 and FTSE CURRENCYCOM:UK100 both rose around 0.2%, as earnings from some of the region's biggest companies kept traders busy.

Germany's DAX futures lagged, down 0.1%, with index heavyweight SAP set to extend losses despite in-line earnings, as ongoing AI-related concerns continued to weigh on sentiment across the software space.

Elsewhere, Deutsche Bank posted its largest annual profit since 2007. Swiss drugmaker Roche reported a 5% rise in earnings but missed expectations as currency effects weighed on drug sales growth, while Sanofi announced a 1 billion euro share buyback.

Other financials also reported results. ING beat profit expectations, boosted by strong interest and fee income, and Swedbank proposed a larger‑than‑expected increase in its annual dividend.

U.S. also futures edged higher.

data.title: Tech Sectors Could Contribute More to Singapore's GDP Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Technology-related sectors could contribute a greater share to Singapore's gross domestic product growth this year compared with 2025, the Monetary Authority of Singapore says in its macroeconomic review. The city-state produces semiconductors essential for electric vehicles and industrial systems, and could benefit from increasing demand for memory chips and autonomous vehicles, says the central bank. Trade-related technology services could post higher sales thanks to artificial intelligence-related demand and an anticipated operating system migration. Meanwhile, the information services sector could see a boost from tech companies' planned investments for data centers and R&D facilities in Southeast Asia, MAS adds. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: ING's Outlook Looks Mixed — Market Talk
data.description: ING's upgraded guidance for 2026 and 2027 is mixed as analyst expectations are already at those levels and it doesn't take into account its Russia exit, Jefferies says in a research note. The Dutch bank guided for 24 billion euros in income this year and 25 billion euros next, but analysts already pencil in 24.2 billion euros and 25.8 billion euros, respectively, analysts write. Other guided metrics, such as fee income, total expenses and return on tangible equity also align with existing consensus expectations. Beyond this, the group posted a decent fourth-quarter print on higher revenues helped by one-off fees and slightly better costs, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Developers Unlikely to Leverage Up Despite Relaxed Debt Rules — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese developers aren't likely to leverage up even after Beijing has eased debt rules, Citi analysts say in a research note. Chinese state media reported that multiple developers were no longer required to submit monthly data related to the "three red lines" policy. However, as most non-state-owned firms have entered debt extension or restructuring, it could take time to resume investment, the analysts say. State-owned firms will comply with requirements on leverage, they note. The move is a possible signal that the deleveraging of the property sector has been accomplished, they note. Citi thinks a policy-driven share-price rebound could be short-lived due to weak sales, price declines and limited improvement in household confidence before prices stabilize. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: REG - Samsung Electronics Samsung Elec - SMSD - Year-End Dividend for FY2025
data.description:
RNS Number : 8909Q Samsung Electronics Co. Ld 29 January 2026  

Year-End Dividend for FY2025

Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. (KS005930, KS005935, SMSN, SMSD) announced that its Board of

Directors, at their meeting on January 28, 2026, approved a dividend plan for the fourth quarter of 2025, payable to the shareholders of record at the close of business on December 31, 2025, as follows:

1. Dividend per share (dividend yield)

- Common shares: KRW 566 (0.5%)

- Preferred shares: KRW 567 (0.7%)

※ Dividend yield is calculated by dividing dividend per share by the average stock price for the week prior to two

   days before the record date based on KRX guidelines.

2. Total payout: KRW 3,753.5 billion

3. Record date: December 31, 2025

※ Dividend payments will be made to only those shareholders who were shareholders of record at the close of

   business on December 31, 2025, as listed on the shareholders' registry on the record date.

4. Other information:

- Number of Independent Directors present at the BOD meeting: 6 (100%)

- Attendance of Audit Committee: Y

- Under the 3-year period shareholder return policy from 2024 to 2026, the annual total amount of

 regular dividend is KRW 9.8 trillion, equivalent to KRW 2.45 trillion per quarter.

- For the current year-end dividend, the Board has resolved to add KRW 1.3 trillion to the regular

 quarterly dividend, resulting in a total KRW 3.75 trillion, considering tax reforms and anticipated

 dividend resources.

- Dividend payments will be made within 30 days of the date of the Annual General Meeting of

Shareholders ("AGM"), in accordance with Article 464 Paragraph 2 of the Commercial Act,

and are subject to the result of audit by an external auditor and approval by a vote of shareholders

at the AGM.

- AGM date is to be determined.

data.title: Vend Marketplaces ASA: Information regarding inspections conducted by the Norwegian Competition Authority with companies in a digital platform market
data.description:
29.1.2026 08:35:53 CET | Vend Marketplaces ASA | Non-regulatory press releases

Reference is made to the announcement published by the Norwegian CompetitionAuthority earlier today.

Vend Marketplaces ASA ("Vend") confirms that the Norwegian CompetitionAuthorityhas conducted inspections at Nettbil's premises in Norway. The Authority hasnotconducted inspections at FINN.

Vend is committed to ensuring that all our companies operate responsibly andincompliance with applicable laws and regulations, and we cooperate fully withtheauthorities.

Out of respect for the Authority's work, we do not comment further on thematter.

Oslo, 29 January 2026Vend Marketplaces ASA

CONTACTS

* Jann-Boje Meinecke, SVP FP&A and Investor Relations, Vend Marketplaces ASA, +47 941 00 835, ir@vend.com * Kristine Eia Kirkholm, Director of Communication, Vend Marketplaces ASA,+47 93 24 78 75, kristine.eia.kirkholm@vend.com * Nathalie Kåvin, Head of Corporate Communication, Vend Marketplaces ASA, nathalie.kavin@vend.com

ABOUT VEND MARKETPLACES ASA

Vend Marketplaces ASA ("Vend") is a family of marketplaces with a strongNordicposition. As a leading marketplaces company within Mobility, Real Estate, Jobsand Recommerce, we provide effortless digital experiences designed for theneedsof tomorrow. We do it with a clear sense of purpose, to create sustainablevalueand long-term growth, for all our stakeholders and society as a whole.

Vend has an ownership share of 14% in Adevinta, a company that was spun off in2019 and is now privately owned by a group of investors.

data.title: easyJet Pretax Loss Widens Despite Increase in Passenger Numbers, Revenue
data.description:
(07:15 GMT) *EasyJet Sees 1Q in Line With Views
(07:15 GMT) *EasyJet Backs FY26 View
(07:16 GMT) *EasyJet: 1Q Headline Pretax Loss GBP93M
(07:17 GMT) *EasyJet: 1Q Headline Pretax Profit of GBP50M for EasyJet Holidays
(07:18 GMT) *EasyJet 1Q Rev GBP2.26B
(07:19 GMT) *EasyJet: Have Seen Continued Demand for Flights and Holidays Over Last Quarter
(07:20 GMT) *EasyJet: Bookings Are Building Well for Summer Season
(07:20 GMT) *EasyJet Says It Had Largest Ever January Booking Period
(07:36 GMT) easyJet Pretax Loss Widens Despite Increase in Passenger Numbers, Revenue

By Cristina Gallardo

EasyJet reported a widened pretax earnings loss despite passengers and revenue rising in the first quarter of its fiscal 2026.

The London-listed budget airline reported Thursday a 93 million pounds ($128.4 million) headline pretax loss for the three months through December. The adjusted metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items, compares with a headline pretax loss of 61 million pounds in the prior-year period.

Revenue, however, grew 11% on year to 2.26 billion pounds, reflecting passenger and ancillary sales growth of 9% each.

The results were in line with expectations, the carrier said.

Demand for flights and holidays continued over the quarter and bookings are building well for the summer season, it said.

The company confirmed its fiscal 2026 guidance.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com
